Graph-Based TF.entity_description: A technique that utilizes graph structures to analyze relationships and recommend items based on their connections.

Coreness.entity_description: Coreness is a graph-based feature that indicates the centrality of nodes within a graph, helping to identify key elements in the dataset.

Strength.entity_description: Strength is a graph-based feature that measures the intensity of connections within a graph, contributing to the analysis of data relationships.

Closeness.entity_description: Closeness is a centrality measure in graph theory that assesses how close a node is to all other nodes in the graph, based on distance.

Reciprocity.entity_description: A measure of the mutual connections between nodes in a network, reflecting the degree of reciprocal relationships.

Eccentricity.entity_description: Eccentricity is a measure in graph theory that describes the greatest distance from a node to any other node in the graph, indicating how far a node is from the 'center' of the graph.
